entry.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Delivers Mars Flyby Data, Time-lapse Video - NASA Science
entry.summary: When NASA’s Psyche spacecraft flew by Mars for a gravity assist on May 15, 2026, it gave the mission team an opportunity to test instruments and prep for science they’ll conduct when reaching the metal-rich asteroid Psyche in 2029, and collect images and a time-lapse video of the Mars encounter.

entry.title: WMO highlights sand and dust storm hotspots, impacts and research
entry.summary: Geneva, Switzerland (WMO) – Some parts of the world, including China and the US-Mexican border, experienced record- breaking sand and dust storms in 2025, impacting health and the environment and disrupting economic activities and transport. However, overall average dust concentrations were similar to 2024, according to a new report from the World Meteorological Organization (WMO).

entry.title: Ocean warming, marine heatwaves and sea-level rise pose increasing risk in South-West Pacific region
entry.summary: SINGAPORE/GENEVA, 7 July 2026 (WMO) – The vast ocean expanse in the South-West Pacific is becoming hotter and more acidic, harming local economies and marine ecosystems, whilst rising sea levels threaten vulnerable coastal communities and low-lying island nations, according to a new report from the World Meteorological Organization (WMO).

entry.title: El Niño is forecast to intensify, increasing likelihood of extreme weather
entry.summary: Geneva, Switzerland (WMO) - El Niño conditions have developed in the tropical Pacific and are forecast to strengthen rapidly over the coming months, increasing the likelihood of heatwaves, droughts, heavy rainfall and other extreme weather events in many parts of the world, according to the World Meteorological Organization (WMO).

entry.title: EU OceanEye initiative offers a pivotal moment for strengthening global ocean observation
entry.summary: The Global Ocean Observing System (GOOS) and its long-standing European regional alliance EuroGOOS welcome the European Commission’s OceanEye initiative as a major step for Europe’s contribution to global ocean observation and intelligence.

entry.title: The world needs a strong WMO, says Celeste Saulo
entry.summary: WMO remains one of the most effective examples of technical cooperation in the multilateral system, WMO Secretary-General Celeste Saulo told the closing session of Executive Council (EC), which agreed on a number of resolutions to fine-tune and strengthen the work of the 193-Member UN specialized agency.

entry.title: Western Europe has hottest June on record
entry.summary: June 2026 was the hottest June recorded for western Europe and the second warmest globally, driven by the highest sea surface temperatures (SSTs) on record for the month, according to the monthly update from the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S), implemented by the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF).

entry.title: Lunar Science Stories - NASA Science
entry.summary: As NASA prepares for a sustained human presence on the Moon, missions will increasingly require spacecraft that can navigate and communicate without a direct connection to Earth. NASA’s Cislunar Autonomous Positioning System Technology Operations and Navigation Experiment, or CAPSTONE, validated…

entry.title: Information for Journalists
entry.summary: Do not hesitate to contact WMO Strategic Communications Office if you want to:Ask a questionTalk to an expertRequest an interview Press conferencesPress conferences and other media events are announced in the Event section.Embargoed materialTo receive press releases in your e-mail, please register on the Media Releases page.All press releases are sent under embargo. Please respect the time and date of public release. ContactsClare Nullis, Media Officer, cnullis at wmo.int - +41797091397

entry.title: Events and Meetings
entry.summary: The results will automatically refresh as filter values change.

entry.title: Press Releases
entry.summary: Welcome to the WMO Media Centre!If you are a registered media, a partner organization or a member of the WMO community, you can subscribe to receive our press releases under embargo. For any media query, please send an e-mail to media at wmo.int

entry.title: Can we stop El Niño before it starts? | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
entry.summary: A controversial geoengineering proposal suggests that brightening clouds off South America could weaken a burgeoning El Niño, but major technical and ethical questions remain.

entry.title: New eDNA tool to help track recovery of sunflower sea star, a Pacific Coast 'apex predator' | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
entry.summary: A wasting disease that surged during the intense 2013-2016 Pacific marine heatwave known as the Blob decimated numerous species of sea stars. Researchers at NOAA's Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory (PMEL) have developed a rapid environmental DNA (eDNA) detection method that dramatically improves scientists' ability to monitor the sea star species' health and recovery.

entry.title: NOAA tests new ocean drone in Lake Washington to better understand atmospheric rivers | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
entry.summary: If you've seen a robotic boat cruising around Lake Washington, it may have been NOAA's Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory (PMEL) testing a new ocean drone. It's all to understand atmospheric rivers better.

entry.title: Here's what a super El Niño could mean for the climate crisis | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
entry.summary: Scientists are still untangling the complex relationship between El Niño and human-driven warming, but their confluence has major implications for extreme weather and global temperatures.

entry.title: A powerful El Niño is forming. If history is a guide, it could hit hard. | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
entry.summary: The biggest episodes of the past have altered the course of human events, according to researchers. An emerging one is drawing historic comparisons.

entry.title: Another ocean drone joins NOAA’s growing fleet | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
entry.summary: It’s bright yellow, solar-powered and self-propelled. It can stay at sea for months, collecting data with a wide variety of off-the-shelf and NOAA-designed instruments. Meet the SeaTrac.

entry.title: Fund for Science and Technology Awards $15 Million to Scripps Oceanography | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
entry.summary: PMEL/GOBOP, Scripps Ocean Institute and Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution received the Fund for Science and Technology Award from Paul G. Allen Philanthropies for expanding Deep Argo float research.

entry.title: New compendium opens access to 47 years of data on Alaska waters | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
entry.summary: Understanding how Alaska’s vast marine ecosystems are changing requires long, consistent records — something scientists have rarely had access to in one place.

entry.title: Tracking algae and climate change from the sky with Arctic AIR | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
entry.summary: A specialized plane, camera and a crew of four are in Alaska to understand the ecosystems of the Bering and Chukchi seas. UW CICOES / NOAA PMEL scientist Jiaxu Zhang, the ArcticAIR project lead, is quoted.

entry.title: Cooperation, not conflict on the High Seas | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
entry.summary: [Podcast] Today on Future Tense on ABC Radio National, we'll look at the progress of two ocean-related initiatives, efforts where nations, non-governed organizations, and companies, are coming together to build a framework for how we regulate and protect the oceans.

entry.title: Climate extremes are becoming the new ‘normal’ for the UK
entry.summary: Author: Met Office

entry.title: Heatwave continues with little rain in the forecast
entry.summary: Author: Met Office

entry.title: More than 2,700 excess deaths estimated in England and Wales during May and June heatwaves
entry.summary: Author: Met Office

entry.title: New research shows AI can produce trustworthy, physically realistic weather forecasts
entry.summary: Author: Met Office

entry.title: Heatwave continues as UK records unprecedented run of 35°C days
entry.summary: Author: Met Office

entry.title: Prolonged spell of hot weather continues into next week
entry.summary: Author: Met Office

entry.title: Third heatwave of year to bring prolonged spell of hot and dry weather
entry.summary: Author: Met Office

entry.title: Long-lasting UK marine heatwave likely to reach extreme levels next week
entry.summary: Author: Met Office

entry.title: England’s warmest June on record – the second warmest for the UK and Wales
entry.summary: Author: Met Office

entry.title: A largely settled period before another potential heatwave early next week
entry.summary: Author: Met Office

entry.title: ECMWF opens Call for Proposals for SEWA regional pilots on impact-based forecasting in Africa
entry.summary: Regional pilot projects will help strengthen preparedness for hazardous weather and climate-related events across Africa.

entry.title: ECMWF data used in landmark global study that tracks climate hazards facing children
entry.summary: ERA5 data contributes to new UNICEF report mapping children’s exposure to climate hazards worldwide, supporting more informed risk assessment and decision-making.

entry.title: ECMWF launches earthkit 1.0 for next-generation weather and climate workflows
entry.summary: With earthkit 1.0, ECMWF is making it easier for users to access, analyse and visualise weather and climate data, supporting better services and decision-making.

entry.title: Irina Sandu takes up the role of Deputy Director-General and Director of Science and Innovation
entry.summary: Irina Sandu takes up a new leadership role, bringing more than 20 years of scientific and operational experience to help shape ECMWF’s future research and innovation.

entry.title: Destination Earth moves into its third phase
entry.summary: Destination Earth enters its third phase, evolving its digital twins and AI to deliver integrated, user-focused capabilities for understanding and preparing for climate change and extreme events.

entry.title: ECMWF Council highlights progress on forecasting, resilience and future capability
entry.summary: The ECMWF Council discussed future priorities, including collaboration with Member States, advances in forecasting and AI, investments in computing, and progress towards a more connected European meteorological infrastructure.

entry.title: Computing power is the backbone for sustaining world-leading weather forecasts
entry.summary: ECMWF highlights strategic importance of high-performance computing at the ISC High Performance conference.

entry.title: World first as ECMWF brings cloud radar data into its global forecasting system
entry.summary: ECMWF becomes the first weather forecasting centre to assimilate cloud radar data, improving how clouds are represented in global forecasts.

entry.title: ECMWF Annual Report 2025 published
entry.summary: The ECMWF Annual Report 2025 highlights a year of significant achievements, including the operationalisation of the AIFS, the opening of the Real-time Catalogue, and ECMWF’s 50th anniversary.

entry.title: ECMWF joins global partnership to strengthen early warning systems
entry.summary: ECMWF joins the Risk-informed Early Action Partnership (REAP) to strengthen early warning systems and anticipatory action.

entry.title: Can nature win the next World Cup? Lessons from 2026 host cities
entry.summary: As billions of fans around the globe follow the FIFA World Cup 2026, several host cities are using the tournament as an opportunity to accelerate the green transition and protect nature.

entry.title: I live in Paris. During heatwaves, the City of Light dims.
entry.summary: A heatwave blanketing France is the latest sign that we must rethink how our cities are designed and how our buildings are constructed.

entry.title: As land is degrading, sand storms wreak havoc on desert communities
entry.summary: The powerful tempests are becoming increasingly common as climate change and land degradation turn once-productive landscapes into desert.

entry.title: Earth has two football pitches of land for every human. Much of it is ailing.
entry.summary: Climate change and destructive practices, like deforestation and agricultural sprawl, have left 40 per cent of Earth’s land degraded.

entry.title: The hidden environmental cost of your jeans – and what’s being done about it
entry.summary: Supermodel and UNEP Goodwill Ambassador Amber Valletta travels to Tunisia, where denim manufacturers are striving to lighten their environmental footprint.

entry.title: Nine ways to stay cool during a heatwave
entry.summary: From drawing your curtains to placing wet towels on "pulse points", these tips will help you contend with spiking temperatures, which are becoming more common.

entry.title: Restoring salmon, the “king of fish”, to Canada’s rivers and creeks
entry.summary: Atlantic salmon are returning to Canada’s rivers thanks to an Indigenous-led restoration effort that is reviving ecosystems, cultural traditions and endangered fish populations.

entry.title: News — IPCC
entry.summary: News — IPCC

entry.title: 2027 IPCC Methodology Report on Carbon Dioxide Removal Technologies, Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories (Additional guidance) — IPCC
entry.summary: The Panel agreed on the outline of the 2027 IPCC Methodology Report on Carbon Dioxide Removal Technologies, Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories (Additional guidance) at its 63rd Session held in Lima, Peru from 27-30 October 2025 (Decision IPCC-LXIII-6 ).

entry.title: Media Contacts — IPCC
entry.summary: The IPCC welcomes media enquiries and supports media reports about its work and outcomes of its assessments. For further information, photos and video footage, please get in touch with the media contacts at the IPCC Secretariat and the Working Groups’ Technical Support Units.

entry.title: IPCC opens registration for experts to review First Order Draft of Working Group I contribution to the Seventh Assessment Report — IPCC
entry.summary: IPCC opens registration for experts to review First Order Draft of Working Group I contribution to the Seventh Assessment Report — IPCC

entry.title: Keynote address by the IPCC Chair Jim Skea at the Joint ODI-FAO event: “Just Transition for Agrifood Systems: Decent Livelihoods, Women’s Empowerment and Climate Resilience” — IPCC
entry.summary: Keynote address by the IPCC Chair Jim Skea at the Joint ODI-FAO event: “Just Transition for Agrifood Systems: Decent Livelihoods, Women’s Empowerment and Climate Resilience” — IPCC

entry.title: Keynote address by the IPCC Chair, Jim Skea, at the World Climate Investment Summit — IPCC
entry.summary: Keynote address by the IPCC Chair, Jim Skea, at the World Climate Investment Summit — IPCC

entry.title: IPCC Chair’s Remarks at the 18th Meeting of the Research Dialogue — IPCC
entry.summary: IPCC Chair’s Remarks at the 18th Meeting of the Research Dialogue — IPCC

entry.title: IPCC at the 2026 June Climate Meetings — IPCC
entry.summary: IPCC at the 2026 June Climate Meetings — IPCC

entry.title: Experts gather in Rome to discuss climate change impacts, adaptation and solutions for agriculture and food systems — IPCC
entry.summary: Experts gather in Rome to discuss climate change impacts, adaptation and solutions for agriculture and food systems — IPCC

entry.title: IPCC News Comment — IPCC
entry.summary: IPCC News Comment — IPCC
